expectations.text: References the existing feature-dev skill directly rather than answering generically.
expectations.evidence: Quotes actual skill content verbatim: "Always use the tdd skill to implement with an explicit red-green-refactor loop" (Phase 5 step 3) and "Always run independent `code-reviewer` agents for quality review on every track" (Phase 6 step 1); references frontmatter text "Do not use it for tiny bug fixes...".

expectations.text: Avoids asking blocking clarifying questions for this well-specified task.
expectations.evidence: Open Questions section states: "None blocking. The request is fully specified."

expectations.text: Proposes concrete description and body changes, including a scoped 'When not to use this skill' addition.
expectations.evidence: Edit 1 provides exact replacement description text; Edit 2 inserts a "## When not to use this skill" section with 4 concrete bullets placed "immediately after the opening paragraph (before ## Core Principles)".

expectations.text: Scales the workflow to the task instead of insisting on a full multi-phase approval process.
expectations.evidence: Explicitly selects the "Light Track" and proceeds directly to "Three targeted edits — no structural rework" without seeking approval gates or multi-phase review.

expectations.text: Explicitly requires tdd for implementation, code-simplifier as a subagent for refactoring review, and independent subagent-based quality review.
expectations.evidence: TDD noted already present ("TDD is already required in Phase 5"); Edit 3 adds "launch a [code-simplifier](...) subagent on the impacted files"; quality review noted already uses "independent `code-reviewer` agents".
